description: Make `goal check` catch an unsafe `...defaults` field as a located [unsafe-default] error, matching `goal build`.

userStories.title: Share the unsafe-zero classifier with sema
userStories.description: As a checker author, I need the unsafe-zero classification (currently zeroSafety in internal/backend/lower.goal) reachable from internal/sema without an import cycle, so the lexical checker can reuse the exact same panic-safety rule the backend enforces.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/sema can call a function that returns the unsafe-zero reason (empty string = safe) for a field type string, using the same rules as backend zeroSafety (nil *T, map, chan, func, method-bearing/named interface, and enum/sealed sum types are unsafe; primitives, structs, slices, arrays, any/error/interface{} are safe).
There is a single definition of that rule shared by both packages (no divergent copy), and internal/sema does not import internal/backend (no import cycle: `go build ./...` succeeds).
`goal build` on a `...defaults` literal that omits a `map[...]` field still exits non-zero with the existing backend message (behavior unchanged).
userStories.notes: zeroSafety is internal/backend/lower.goal:438 (typ, decls, info, depth). backend imports sema, so sema cannot import backend — move the classifier to a package both import (or into sema and have backend call it). Preserve the reason strings the backend error already shows.

userStories.title: Add lexical [unsafe-default] check in sema
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need the lexical checker to flag a `...defaults` literal whose omitted fields have panic-prone zero values, so the violation surfaces as a first-class diagnostic instead of only a transpile failure.
acceptanceCriteria: For a `T{ ..., ...defaults }` literal, the sema check emits one Severity.Error Diagnostic with Code "unsafe-default" per omitted field whose zero is unsafe, positioned at the `...defaults` spread (line:col), naming the field and its type.

A field that is explicitly set in the literal (including a `Field: _` skip) produces no unsafe-default diagnostic; a `...defaults` literal whose omitted fields are all safe produces no unsafe-default diagnostic.

The new check is invoked by the sema Check aggregator (the same path `goal check`'s lexical stage runs), and "unsafe-default" is registered in internal/guide/catalog.goal for feature 08-no-zero-value with severity error.
userStories.notes: Mirror the sibling in internal/sema/fields.goal (CheckFields emits missing-field Error and unresolved-literal-type Warning). CheckFields already skips `...defaults` literals for missing-field; the new check is the complement over those same literals. Reuse the US-001 classifier. Some tests assert every emitted Code has a guide catalog entry, so add the catalog row here to keep task check green.

userStories.title: Prove goal check fails and document it
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need on-disk fixtures and docs proving `goal check` now rejects an unsafe `...defaults` at the CLI, so the guarantee is regression-tested and discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: `go run ./cmd/goal check <fixture>` on a `...defaults` literal omitting a nil-map (or nil-pointer) field exits 1 and prints `[unsafe-default]` with a file:line:col location; the same file with that field set exits 0.

A testdata/check/08-no-zero-value case with a `// want [unsafe-default]` marker exists and passes; corpus/manifest.json is regenerated (go run ./cmd/corpus-gen) and the hardcoded check-case count in internal/corpus/generate_test.go is updated to match.
features/08-no-zero-value/SYNTAX.md (and DECISIONS.md) record that an unsafe `...defaults` is an [unsafe-default] checker error caught by `goal check`, not only by `goal build`.
userStories.notes: The corpus check runner is TestTranspileRunner over corpus/manifest.json; check cases live under testdata/check with `// want [code]` markers (see internal/corpus/check_runner.go). TestGenerateCounts (internal/corpus/generate_test.go) asserts check == 50 today — bump it when the new case lands, as US-039's T6 bumped the transpile count.
